I can see the gray shape has overlapped with the yellow shape! The gray shape now has some yellow pixels visible, and there are yellow dots inside the gray shape. This suggests the gray shape is now overlapping with the yellow shape.
Let me check the current positions.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] >= 50:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"Gray (c): row {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, col {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
    if n['color'] == 'B' and n['pixels'] >= 50:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"B shape: row {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, col {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
    if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] >= 10:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"Yellow: row {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, col {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Count all nodes
print(f"\nTotal nodes: {len(seg['nodes'])}")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, hash={n['hash']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
